channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Pakistan VPN ban: how to stay safe online and bypass restrictions
item.description: Authorities are set to implement a new VPN policy to crackdown on "unregistered" services. Here some workarounds and alternatives.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Home Entertainment Holiday Specials: Top Deals on Streaming Devices, TVs, and More
item.description: Get ready for the holiday season with these amazing deals on streaming devices, TVs, projectors, and speakers.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Creature Commandos: DCU's First Project Aims to Set Tone for Rebooted Universe
item.description: Dean Lorey discusses his excitement over helming the first-ever project in James Gunn and Peter Safran's rebooted DC Cinematic Universe.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Roundup of Tech News: OpenAI Sora Leaked, Apple's Favorite Apps Revealed
item.description: Summary of top tech news stories from around the world, including leaked Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra, Spotify Wrapped 2024, and Apple's favorite apps of 2024.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Artificial intelligence changes across the US
item.description: As more companies implement artificial intelligence for daily tasks, some industry leaders are working to push back against certain aspects of the technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The Tech Ecosystem Powering Latin America’s Growth
item.description: Even for those of us optimistic about Latin America’s (LATAM) business prospects, the path ahead isn’t without challenges. Political tensions persist. Several major economies in the region continue to seek balance and stability.
item.dc:creator: Nacho De Marco, Fast Company

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online advertising
item.description: The Competition Bureau of Canada has filed an antitrust lawsuit against Google, accusing the tech giant of abusing its dominant position in the online advertising technology market to stifle competition and harm advertisers, publishers, and consumers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australian Parliament bans social media for under-16s with world-first law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A social media ban for children under 16 passed the Australian Parliament on Friday in a world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online ads
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's antitrust watchdog said Thursday it is suing Google over alleged anticompetitive conduct in the tech giant’s online advertising business and wants the company to sell off two of its ad tech services and pay a penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: FTC opens antitrust investigation into Microsoft’s cloud, AI, and cybersecurity practices
item.description: The US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has launched an antitrust investigation into Microsoft, scrutinizing its cloud computing, software licensing, cybersecurity, and AI businesses. The inquiry follows over a year of informal interviews with competitors and business partners, Bloomberg reported.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Intel’s $7.9B subsidy deal comes at a high price for the chipmaker
item.description: Intel’s $7.86 billion subsidy agreement with the US government imposes strict conditions on the company’s ability to divest stakes in its chipmaking division if the unit becomes independent.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Copilot Studio Agents get a major upgrade via Microsoft 365 Copilot
item.description: Microsoft Ignite 2024 put an end to Microsoft’s dalliance with chatbots. Not because the company is walking away from natural language interfaces, but because it’s taking what it learned with its first Copilots and refocusing on tangible productivity gains from large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to use ref structs in C# 13
item.description: In the C# programming language, structs or structure types are value types that allow faster access because they are usually stored in the stack.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How Biden’s AI tech could play a role in Trump’s immigration crackdown
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will return to power next year with a raft of technological tools at his disposal that would help deliver his campaign promise of cracking down on immigration—among them, surveillance and artificial intelligence technology that the Biden administration already uses to he
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Coast Guard adds new cyber requirements for ship-to-shore cranes
item.description: The directive builds upon a mandate issued in February — the public notice doesn’t disclose specifics of the new requirements. The post Coast Guard adds new cyber requirements for ship-to-shore cranes first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Johnson to host Musk, Ramaswamy at Capitol ahead of DOGE work
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) will host Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy, the co-leads of the newly established "Department of Government Efficiency" (DOGE), at the Capitol on Dec. 5, he announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Kai Trump shows Elon Musk awkwardly tutoring Trump in rocket science
item.description: While Trump's aspiring influencer granddaughter happily shared video of her joining him at Musk's latest SpaceX launch, she also revealed moments of the president-elect looking 'out of depth' and 'intimidated.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Fox host: Podcasters like Rogan have 'earned' seats in White House briefing room
item.description: Fox News host Will Cain said popular podcasters like Joe Rogan have “earned” seats in the White House briefing room. When discussing the potential for a shake-up in the James S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Amazon's $4B bet on an AI startup
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australia's social media ban for kids is closer to becoming law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia’s House of Representatives on Wednesday passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years old from social media, leaving it to the Senate to finalize the world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Windows Recall: All your privacy questions answered
item.description: Windows Recall is an AI-based feature that will be built into new Copilot+ PCs. Recall takes snapshots of your screen every few seconds and uses that data to make a searchable index of everything you’ve ever done on your PC.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Kotlin for Java developers: Concurrency with coroutines
item.description: The ability to perform multiple operations simultaneously is one of the most important aspects of any programming language. Orchestrating multiple paths of execution is inherently complex and there are various approaches to taming this complexity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Starbucks and other retailers hit with ongoing ransomware attack on software provider
item.description: Major supply-chain software provider Blue Yonder is working to restore its systems after a ransomware attack hit the Panasonic-owned firm last week. Blue Yonder, which counts Starbucks, major U.K.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Equinix to cut 3% of staff amidst the greatest demand for data center infrastructure ever
item.description: Even as demand for data infrastructure surges to an all-time high, Equinix is planning to lay off 3% of its workforce, suggesting a growing skills mismatch in the industry.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to (kindly) ask people not to post your kids' photos on social media
item.description: With kids facing a seemingly increasing amount of threats from creeps on the internet, here's how you can respectfully ask people not to post photos of your children on social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
